input:
Please summarize the goals for scientists in this text:

Within three days, the intertwined cup nest of grasses was complete, featuring a canopy of overhanging grasses to conceal it. And decades later, it served as Rinkert’s portal to the past inside the California Academy of Sciences. Information gleaned from such nests, woven long ago from species in plant communities called transitional habitat, could help restore the shoreline in the future. Transitional habitat has nearly disappeared from the San Francisco Bay, and scientists need a clearer picture of its original species composition—which was never properly documented. With that insight, conservation research groups like the San Francisco Bay Bird Observatory can help guide best practices when restoring the native habitat that has long served as critical refuge for imperiled birds and animals as adjacent marshes flood more with rising sea levels. “We can’t ask restoration ecologists to plant nonnative species or to just take their best guess and throw things out there,” says Rinkert.
reference_output: Scientists are studying nests hoping to learn about transitional habitats that could help restore the shoreline of San Francisco Bay.

input: Write a news style post about a fake event, like aliens from Mars landing on Earth. It is meant to be funny but also be written in the authoritative style of a news report, kind of like The Onion.
reference_output:
Today marks a new day in not only American history but world history. We are no longer alone in the universe. Life outside our planet exists and aliens have landed in Nevada.

NASA scientists had been tracking a strange non-terrestrial object for days leading up to today's big event. The object appeared from behind the dark side of the moon and was originally believed to be an untracked asteroid or comet. However, the object's path and speed started changing until today it entered Earth's atmosphere. Instead of burning up like a hunk of space rock, it was able to penetrate the atmosphere and survive.

The military scrambled jets and the first sighting described the flying object as being triangular with a matte black exterior and no visible ports of entry or egress. It was a tense few moments as first contact was made but the craft was allowed to continue unhindered as it showed no hostile intent.

Passing over Washington, D.C., Chicago, and other major metropolitan areas the craft finally touched down in Nevada. Many top officials believed they were heading to the notorious Area 51. But that proved not to be the case as the UFO instead landed directly on the Vegas strip. Roughly the size of a 747 jet, all traffic stopped immediately.

Two figures exited the craft distinctly humanoid in shape. The taller of the two raised its right hand and made a gesture splitting their fingers into a V-shape and spoke in perfect English, "Live long and prosper." It was at this point that the shorter alien slapped the taller alien on the shoulder and let out an audible sigh.

They were Trekkies. The aliens were Star Trek fans.

As people approached the aliens they noticed they were clad in the uniforms of the original Star Trek officers, one in yellow for command and one in blue for science specialty. This was no alien invasion, this was a vacation.

The aliens were allowed to proceed as journalists and military personnel alike shadowed them. They went directly to the casino already possessing American dollars and proceeded to lose it all at the dice table in less than an hour. Following this display, they went to the buffet and ate three plates of crab legs. These visitors to Vegas were not too different from us. We are not alone.

input: Write a funny, short story about someone who will stop at nothing to go green and reduce their carbon footprint.
reference_output:
Garry has a real green thumb, and taking care of the environment is extremely important to him. He takes every possible step to cut his carbon footprint, and it inspires and mystifies everyone around him in equal measure. Each night before he goes to bed, he cuts off the power to his house so that his electronic devices don’t draw idle power. 

Almost all of his appliances are quite old, as Garry wants to get as much use as possible out of them before contributing to new pollution-generating production. The only exceptions are his new, water-efficient dishwasher and his solar-powered hot water tank. 

The Homeowner’s Association hates Garry because he grows corn on his front lawn. All of his food is locally sourced as much as possible, and he commutes to work on a bike that doesn’t look like it should work. He built it himself out of recycled parts, and it seems to jump and rock like Fred Flinstone’s car. Yet, it stays in one piece and carries him faithfully to work without contributing to pollution. All in all, there’s nothing Garry won’t do to keep his carbon footprint small.

input:
Write a letter to the Editor responding to the news article, as if you are a dog. Thank the editor for highlighting this important topic. Rewrite all the main facts in the article.

A vet has warned pet owners to be very careful when using oil diffusers in their homes, as certain oils can prove fatal for dogs.
This comes after a grandmother shared how her beloved pooch was poisoned by the oils in her diffuser, and was left unable to recognise her when she returned from working a late shift as a nanny.
Sharing her warning on Facebook, Marrianne Whyte explained that she'd initially assumed she'd woken her dog up from a dream, in which he had been experiencing night terrors, but found he was "still acting weird" the next day.
After turning off her new diffuser, Marrianne noticed that her pet's condition started to improve, but didn't make the connection straight away. Marrianne set off for another shift, but ended up returning home early after receiving a worrying call from her dog sitter.
In her post, which has been shared more than 1.5 million times, Marrianne, who lives in New Zealand, wrote: "[She] said that he wouldn't come out from under the bed. It was very odd as he is a happy dog.
"I came from work early and again, he was very confused about who I was. So I took him to an emergency vet. It turns out that the tea tree oil I was using in the diffuser is toxic for dogs."

Thankfully, tests confirmed that Marriane's dog hadn't suffered liver damage, but they "weren't out of the woods yet", and the vet gave him fluids under his skin to get rid of any toxins.
Vet clinic Pets on Parker told 7life that cats are also at risk of being poisoned by essential oils because they don't have a liver enzyme called glucuronyl transferase, which is required to break them down.
The experts explained: "Their livers are slower at eliminating these compounds, which can lead to toxic build-up. While dogs do not lack this enzyme, they are still sensitive to essential oils and can become very sick if exposed to them.

"Common signs of poisoning as a result of inhalation (usually from an oil diffuser) include laboured breathing, lack of coordination and difficulty walking, drooling, diarrhoea, lethargy, depression, low body temperature, weakness, muscle tremors, and vomiting."
reference_output:
Dear Editor,

Many thanks for raising this important issue in your paper. Dog owners must be very careful when using oil diffusers in their homes, particularly ones containing tea tree oil. Tea tree oil can be lethal for dogs.

A New Zealand grandmother shared a heartbreaking story about how her beloved pooch was poisoned by the oils in her diffuser. The poor dog was left unable to recognise her when she returned from working a late shift as a nanny. It is great that Marrianne Whyte shared her warning on Facebook because it raises awareness of this issue. In fact, her post has been shared 1.5 million times.

When she tried to wake her dog up, she thought the dog had been having a dream, or been experiencing night terrors. However, she found that he was "still acting weird" the next day. The following day Marrianne set off for another shift but had to return home early after receiving a worrying call from her dog sitter because the dog wouldn't come out from under the bed. It was very out of character because he was a happy dog. After returning early from work Marrianne Whyte found the dog in a confused state, so she took him to the emergency vet. 

It was then she discovered that the tea tree oil in her diffuser is toxic for dogs. There is a happy ending to the story, because fortunately, tests confirmed that Marriane's dog hadn't suffered liver damage. However, the vet gave him fluids under his skin to get rid of any toxins. Cat lovers should also be aware, as Vet Clinic Pets on Parker, so rightly points out. Cats are also at risk of being poisoned by essential oils. Cats don't have a liver enzyme called glucuronyl transferase, which is required to break them down. Cats' livers are slower at eliminating these compounds, which can lead to toxic build-up. 

Dogs, like me, do not lack this enzyme, but we are still sensitive to essential oils and we are liable to become very sick if exposed to them. Owners can look out for common signs of poisoning as a result of inhalation from an oil diffuser, by watching for laboured breathing, lack of coordination and difficulty walking, drooling, diarrhoea, lethargy, depression, low body temperature, weakness, muscle tremors and vomiting.

Therefore, you see the terrible effects that these diffusers can have on animals like me. I thank you for highlighting and raising awareness of this problem. I hope that no other dog will have to suffer as a result of tea tree oil diffusers.

Regards,
Rolo the dog

input: I'm going to write a psychological mystery. I want you to craft a scene that sets up the female lead, who is 17 and was picked up by a detective who wants to take her to the station so they can help her. She is wearing light makeup and a dress, but no shoes. Most notably, her expression is complete dead like there's no life in her. The detective, Rustage, plans to pass her off to someone at the station, but life has a funny way of twisting things. I want the tone to be a bit grim and mysterious. Make it sound literary as well. name the girl Talia.
reference_output:
Her name was Talia, it seemed. She was probably about five-six with long, straight black hair. Her hair looked like it was probably silky, but at that moment, it was a bit of a frazzled mess. She wore a tight-fitting purple dress that accentuated her slim figure and slight bust. She wasn't wearing shoes or socks, and when she stepped, one could see the black caked on the soles of her feet. Surprisingly, she didn't wear heavy makeup. It was light and natural, with mascara, a light blush around her cheeks, and subtle lip stain being the only adornments. 

These, of course, were not her most notable features. When she walked into the dinner, her eyes were hollow. They held no shine--no recognition of life, even. If there was someone behind that dull glare, she was certainly preoccupied with other things. No amount of beauty or makeup would have hidden away the hideous truths that were buried beneath her.

However, that didn't seem to be the purpose of her attire or makeup. To Rustage, it didn't seem that she had any purpose at all, but he couldn't simply leave her alone. Something told him there was a story there that needed to be uncovered. It wouldn't be his job. He only handled the strangest, most obscure cases that weren't for the light of heart or sane. It wasn't right of him to pass a child who was clearly in need. He would take her back to the station and let them take care of her. That was his plan, at least. Things don't always go according to plan, though.

input: Why is Aberdeen called the Granite City?
reference_output: Alberdeen's nickname, the Granite City, comes from the city's history and contribution of granite, in addition to the architectural style of more than fifty percent of the city's buildings. Many of the city's most popular buildings and residential properties were built using granite from large quarries around the Nort-East, most notably the Rubislaw Quarry in Aberdeen's west end. As technology and practices for handling a large amount of rock improved, Aberdeen quickly became the granite capital of the world, exporting granite by sea to several areas globally.

input:
Answer the following questions using the passage below:
Who was Harry Potter written by?
Who originally published the Harry Potter series?

Harry Potter is a series of seven fantasy novels written by British author J. K. Rowling. The novels chronicle the lives of a young wizard, Harry Potter, and his friends Hermione Granger and Ron Weasley, all of whom are students at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry. The main story arc concerns Harry's conflict with Lord Voldemort, a dark wizard who intends to become immortal, overthrow the wizard governing body known as the Ministry of Magic and subjugate all wizards and Muggles (non-magical people).

The series was originally published in English by Bloomsbury in the United Kingdom and Scholastic Press in the United States. All versions around the world are printed by Grafica Veneta in Italy.[1] A series of many genres, including fantasy, drama, coming-of-age fiction, and the British school story (which includes elements of mystery, thriller, adventure, horror, and romance), the world of Harry Potter explores numerous themes and includes many cultural meanings and references.[2] According to Rowling, the main theme is death.[3] Other major themes in the series include prejudice, corruption, and madness.[4]
reference_output: Harry Potter was written by British author J. K. Rowling. The series was originally published in the UK by Bloomsbury and in the US by Scholastic Press.

input: Compose a 4 stanza poem about the beauty of the natural world, using vivid imagery and sensory language. Please make the tone contemplative and peaceful.

I love to watch the leaves unfurl,
To see the buds break into flower,
To feel the warmth of the sun on my skin,
And the coolness of the breeze in my hair.

I love to hear the birds sing in the trees,
To smell the flowers in bloom,
To taste the sweetness of the fruit,
And to feel the softness of the grass beneath my feet.

input:
In short, why does the article say second terms are terrible?

Warning: second terms are terrible. Now, the bad news for Democrats. Beginning with George Washington, second terms are a litany of troubles. No modern president has had a good time of it. Work backwards: George Bush’s second term started with Katrina and ended in financial disaster; Clinton met Monica and was impeached; Reagan ran into Iran Contra, saw his approval plummet, lost the Senate, had a bout with cancer, and grew deeply despondent. Richard Nixon resigned. Eisenhower suffered a heart attack, a stroke, and became enmeshed in civil rights. FDR lost his coalition in Congress, saw the economy falter, and was humiliated when he tried to intervene in the midterm elections. Of course, history is not destiny. But it offers second-termers a gloomy warning. It gets only harder and the burdens of office take a personal toll.
reference_output: In short, second terms have been historically tough for a lot of past presidents. Many of the events were out of the control of the president at the time, but nevertheless, they ran into problems that impeded the second term of their presidency. Events such as natural disasters, scandals, sickness, economic collapse, and more have all been issues during the second term of presidencies.

input: I love reading, but I have a hard time retaining what I read, especially nonfiction books. Can you give me several techniques to try in 175 words or less?
reference_output:
1. Read with a reason. Why did you choose the book you have? Chances are, it was relevant and meaningful to you. Don't just read something for the sake of reading or because someone said you should.
2. Write in the margins. Write your thoughts as they happen to make them more memorable and significant. Writing down your thoughts helps commit them to memory.
3. Take a rest. Put your book away for a couple of weeks after completion and let your mind mull over the content.
4. Consider how you're paying attention. When you read, focus only on reading. Don't let distractions get in the way. Put your phone in another room and sit somewhere comfortable and quiet so you can pay better attention to the page in front of you.
5. Schedule around your reading time. Instead of fitting in time for reading in your already busy schedule, do it the other way around. Set aside time for reading first, and then schedule chores, errands, etc. around it.

input: Write a 300-word blog post about taking care of a guinea pig.
reference_output: If you're looking for information on how to care for a guinea pig, you've come to the right place! Guinea pigs are animals that are best in bonded pairs or groups. You should never adopt only one guinea pig as they are social creatures. Guinea pigs need to eat hay (timothy, oat, or wheatgrass) every day as their teeth are constantly growing and need to be ground down as they chew. They should also have nutritional pellets daily and a mixture of vegetables. Never feed your guinea pig iceberg lettuce, though, as it's too watery! When your guinea pig learns its feeding pattern, you'll notice a high-pitched 'wheek!' sound coming from their cage as soon as the fridge opens or they hear the rustle of a plastic bag! Supplement your feedings with occasional Vitamin C drops, also. Take your guinea pigs out once a day for free roam or floor time so that they can stretch their legs and play. Cages should be at minimum 2 feet by 3 feet for a single guinea pig - but more is always better if you've got the space. Boars need to have boar cleanings once a month where you'll clean out their inside sac of all the hay, food, and bedding particles that accumulate in there. Speaking of bedding - many owners recommend using fabric cage liners for guinea pigs as it's soft on their feet, easy to clean, and good for the environment. These include a top and bottom layer of fleece with an absorbing pad in the middle. You'll just wipe up quick messes of pee or poop on top, then throw the liners in the washing machine once a week. If you can't do liners, soft untreated paper bedding is best.

input: I’m looking to visit Berlin but have no clue what neighborhoods to stay in. I’ll be on a bit of a budget while there, what are some good neighborhoods to check out?
reference_output:
- Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf: This is the best area for a family to stay. It used to be its own town, separate from the city. There are a ton of historical sites here, like the Charlottenburg Palace, and it’s a very safe part of the city.

- Kreuzberg: Kreuzberg is the best place to go if you want nightlife. There are tons of bars and clubs that are open until the morning comes. There’s a lot to do during the day here as well, from historical locations, parks, and museums; you’ll have plenty to do and check out.

- Mitte: Mitte is home to many of Berlin’s must-see sites and is the best neighborhood for sightseeing. Mitte also has the famous Museum Island. Mitte also has tons of great restaurants and bars. It’s a great neighborhood for first-time visitors that want to see all of the major attractions the city has to offer.

- Prenzlauer Berg: Prenzlauer Berg is one of the coolest neighborhoods in Berlin, home to people from all walks of life. There’s great access to the City Center, tons of Cafes, and the Berlin Wall Memorial.

- Friedrichshain: Friedrichshain is the best part of the city to go if you’re looking for nightlife. It’s also full of awesome street art and is one of the more affordable parts of the city. If you’re into art and looking to party and be on a budget, this is the part of the city for you.

input: A fairy came to a man with 5 gifts: Fame, Love, Riches, Pleasure, and Death, The man could choose one thing to take and he chose Pleasure. Continue the story while expanding on what the main character of the story did after he chose the gift of Pleasure. Expand on what made him realize it was the wrong choice to choose Pleasure. Highlight the excitement he had about his gift initially but also the emptiness and disappointment it later brought. Write the story in bullet points.
reference_output:
- I finally had it. The thing I have been looking for my entire life was finally in the palm of my hands. I wasn't going to let this go to waste; I wanted to use it quickly.

- I had many options and many paths I could take. Would I go on vacation and soak up the sun? Would I quit my job and leave my life behind to start fresh?

- I decided that I would first visit the most popular club I could find where the most famous celebrities would be found. Maybe I would spend a few weeks away from home. 

- I left my family and the party ensued. I would finally achieve all that I'd been working and searching for.

- The days and nights came and went and I awoke to the sound of screeching tires. It was 4 am and I was in the driver's seat of my car that was now totaled.

- I could not remember how I had gotten there or how many days I had been partying. All I knew now was I had no car, I was going to jail for a few nights, and I have not spoken to my family.

- That was 3 weeks that are now wiped from my memory. What I thought would give me a new life brought me despair and complete isolation. If I could choose a different path, I would.

input: Compose a brief tale about a toilet that can see ghosts. Keep it between 200 and 300 words.
reference_output:
There was once a toilet that lived in an old, enormous mansion. The mansion had been long abandoned but was never torn down due to its prominent history. No one lived in the mansion, but the home was open to the public for tours. The toilet had been there for centuries and had seen thousands of people wander through the mansion's many hallways. 

One day, the toilet saw a person walking through the hallway. The toilet looked out the window and saw that it was nighttime, and the mansion was not open for tours during the night. Confused, the toilet intently watched the person walk into the bathroom. They closed the door, glanced in the mirror, and fixed their hair. The toilet watched this peculiar behavior and was shocked as the person walked through the door without opening it and disappeared on the other side.

It was on that day that the toilet realized he was able to see ghosts. This was the first time it had ever seen a ghost in its life, and the toilet began to wonder if there were more ghosts in the mansion and if so, how long they have lived there. The toilet patiently waited until night to see the ghost again. Around the same time, another ghost appeared suddenly in the bathroom and adjusted its tie in the mirror. The toilet was absolutely astounded and wondered if all the people in the supposed tours that he had seen over the years were real people or ghosts.

input: Come up with a short story about a man teaching his young son how to fix a vehicle, where the father wants to appear smart for his son but doesn't know much about fixing cars.
reference_output:
Jeff watched as his father got under the hood of the family sedan: a 15-year-old 4-door vehicle that had been with the family as long as he could remember. Jeff's father, a proud man in his 50s, looked intently at the complex machinery under the hood as if examining a complicated puzzle.

Jeff stood next to his father and leaned over to see what his father was seeing. "What's wrong with it, Dad?" he asked him.

"Not sure," he replied, "but I think it has something to do with the engine. There's no reason why it should just stop like that unless it were the engine. Do me a favor and get in the driver's seat and try starting it."

Jeff, eager to start learning to drive, jumped into the driver's seat of the sedan and turned the key in the ignition. Nothing. He tried again. Nothing. Jeff exited the vehicle and walked back to his dad. "What do you think it is?" he asked.

Jeff's father began touching things on the engine, seemingly actively investigating the source of the vehicle's problems. He pulled on some hoses and checked the fluid levels in all the fluid-containing reservoirs, then sighed. "Hmm," he began a bit puzzled, "This is interesting."

"What is it?" Jeff asked, thinking his father had identified the problem. His father, without missing a beat, replied, "The windshield washer fluid is low." Confused by the reply, Jeff wondered what the connection was between the windshield washer fluid being low and the engine not turning over.

Jeff's father felt defeated and decided to call the nearest tow truck to get the vehicle towed back home. When the tow truck driver arrived, he looked at the vehicle and Jeff's father peering under the hood. "Can you try turning the engine over please?" he asked Jeff's father. "We tried that," he replied but obliged the driver anyway.

"Okay," the driver said unamused, "Did you make sure there was gas in the tank?" Jeff looked up at his dad in amazement. "Did you get gas yesterday after work like Mom told you to?" he asked incredulously. Jeff's father, now with a look of realization on his face, looked at the driver and shook his head. "Oh my god," he said in shock. The driver laughed uncontrollably.

Dotty woke up feeling well-rested. The storm last night had been pretty frightful, and the pasture was beset with rain and thunder the entire night. As Dotty went around her pen, she noticed something that caused her to stop dead in her tracks. The fence was down, likely due to the storm last night. Dotty was terrified; the fence wasn't supposed to be down. Now, all manner of scary creatures could get inside and eat it. It was this thought that triggered a radically opposite one: she could explore outside the pen. Finally, get to see what was in those woods on the other side of the street.

Now that she thought about it, she was convinced to try it. She took the brave first step over the broken plank and made her way toward the road. It was still early morning. The farmer wouldn't even notice she'd gone for the morning, and besides, she would be back within a few hours. She took her first step onto the road, noting just how much firmer it was than the soft grass of the pasture. All of a sudden, two glowing eyes appeared from down the street, growing larger as they approached. A terrifying horn sounded, and Dotty froze, unable to move. The truck veered to the left around her, whooshing past all at once.

Once the truck had gone, she found herself again and bolted back inside the pen. 
"Well," she thought, "that's an experience I never need to have again!"

When Passiontulip mentioned that she was hungry, they ran down to the beach to search for food that tourists might have discarded.

“There are lots of kids down here today,” Beaches said to her friend. 

“They’re noisy!” Jim-Jim replied with a frowny face.

The girl pointed at the white rhinestone collar worn by the white kitty and all the kids had to agree that she was not a stray.

input:
Summarize what hi-fi audio is in less than three sentences.

What is hi-fi audio?
Hi-fi audio, also known as high fidelity audio, is simply audio that is very high quality.

The factors that play into this level of quality are a high accuracy at reproducing the audible frequencies in audio, and extremely little distortion in the original audio signal. This in general is what “high fidelity” means.

Let’s break this down in another way. You’ve likely heard of lo-fi music before; if not, it’s a genre that is often mixed with elements of jazz or hip-hop. The instrumentation is not relevant for this example, however; let’s focus on the general sound.

Lo-fi music is characterized as having a low quality, almost underwater type sound, usually with crackling and warping sounds from a vinyl record, to add to the ambience. Instruments are usually “broken” sounding or boxy.

With hi-fi, it’s simply the opposite of lo-fi. If lo-fi audio is described as being old, broken, and fuzzy sounding, hi-fi is accurately described as sounding clear, clean, crisp, and free from distortion and other unwanted sonic artifacts.

A more technical answer would be this:

Music files that have a higher sample rate and/or bit depth than CDs are what high fidelity audio is considered to be.

CDs have a sample rate of 44.1 KHz and a 16-bit bit depth, so as long as at least one of these numbers is higher than those of a CD, audio is considered to be hi-fi.
reference_output: Hi-fi audio—as opposed to lo-fi, which a defining characteristic of is its low-quality sound—is high-quality audio with exceptional frequency response and little if any distortion. More technically, it refers to audio with a sample rate higher than 44.1 kHz and 16-bit bit depth.

input:
Reframe this tutorial as someone describing their starting experiences with music production. Expand on some of the experiences like mixing and mastering music.

Starting music production from fresh is an overwhelming experience for most people. It certainly was for us! But don't worry, it's really easy to get involved in the world of production.

If you want to get into the audio engineer route, go volunteer at studios. Try to get a job as a runner, or a tea/coffee boy. If you stay there long enough eventually you'll be taught things and climb the ranks.

If you just want to make beats in your bedroom, then the first thing you should do is download a free DAW, or pay for one. Next, learn the basics of your DAW, and start to try and make a song. It doesn't matter how bad the song is, or how far away it sounds from what's in your head. Just start doing it.

When you run into something that you don't know how to do, or understand, head to YouTube and watch some tutorials on it.

Once you've finished a song, attempt to mix it down, and attempt to master it after that.

First start off by just using volume levelling to mix your songs, and get a feel for what level each instrument should be at. Then start learning what compression is, what EQ is etc.

Make sure to watch tutorials on how to mix certain instruments, like vocals, or how to use certain tools you don't understand. But, don't get stuck in a rut of watching tutorials and not doing anything.

The best way you will learn is by doing.

After making your first couple songs, start to learn about sound design. Choose a synth, and learn it inside and out. We recommend Serum for learning, as it's beginner friendly and a powerhouse of a synth.

Keep making new songs, and trying out the new things you have learned from tutorials. You will improve rapidly by doing this. A good thing to do is try to separate sessions and spend different days of the week doing different things to improve your learning.
reference_output:
I had no experience when I started trying to make music, so I was super overwhelmed by all of my options. I had a lot of possible avenues to take, so it was a bit hard to come to a decision. I wanted to start small, so I just downloaded a DAW and started going at it, watching tutorials online to help me find my way. It was rough: I couldn't make anything I liked the sound of, I couldn't really capture the rhythms or melodies in my head, or they'd leave me before I had the opportunity to get them down. Not only did I have to worry about all of those things, but there were also all the complicated things like EQ, mixing, mastering, and a bunch of other stuff I didn't really understand. It was hard to find a balance, both literally (getting the levels of instruments and vocals to sound right), and figuratively (finding out what to do and putting that into practice).

It was a struggle pushing through all of that, but it kind of got easier as time went on. I started trying to learn a bit more about sound design with Serum, a synthesizer that's both easy to understand and really good at what it's meant for. I kept at it, trying to make more songs and improve, and it's steadily worked. Something that helped me out was trying different techniques or practices on a given day, which helped me focus on various skills I needed to learn.

input:
I have 2 lists below. Can you show me how to make a bar chart from them using Python?

list1 = ["USA", "China", "Russia", "Brazil", "Japan"]
list2 = [22, 53, 32, 45, 56]
reference_output:
There is a useful module called MatPlotLib that can help you achieve this. If you have not already, you will need to install the module first.

To install it with PIP, open your command line and enter:
pip install matplotlib

To install it with Conda, enter:
conda install -c conda-forge matplotlib

Then you can import the module into your Python code. Here is the full code:
```python
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt

list1 = ["USA", "China", "Russia", "Brazil", "Japan"]
list2 = [22, 53, 32, 45, 56]

plt.bar(list1, list2)
plt.show()
```

This will display a bar chart with list1 on the x-axis and list2 on the y-axis.

input: Hey. I need an introductory paragraph or two for a blog article about building interesting D&D encounters using timers (like rolling a d4 and saying something will happen in that many rounds). reference_output:
Consider this scenario: a party of adventurers walks into a room and encounters several high HP monsters. The monsters are not particularly dangerous, but they do take a while to put down. After the fight, the party finds a locked door. It takes a few tries, but the rogue eventually gets it open. No biggie. It's not like they have a time limit.

As those familiar with ICRPG may already be aware, adding timers to this encounter can make it much more interesting. For instance, the high HP monsters might suddenly become a meaningful obstacle if the party has to stop one of them from completing a ritual. input: Write an article about online banking. Give some tidbits of information that readers might not know. Talk about all the benefits of online banking. Keep the word count under 250 words.

The idea of being able to bank anytime, anywhere appeals to most consumers because the average person often has a number of activities to attend to on any given day that can include taking kids to school and picking them up, shopping for groceries, cooking, cleaning, laundry, homework, and working eight hours per day. The ability to quickly and efficiently get the banking done means you can mark one very important item off the list.  

Online Banking benefits the bank and its employees as well. Tasks that could once only be completed by a bank employee are now quickly and easily handled by the customer at their convenience. Because of these and other advantages, online banking continues to grow into a multi-billion dollar business. input: Come up with a theory that supports the idea that the universe is contained within a gigantic black hole.
reference_output:
Although current scientific evidence and belief does not support it, there is some merit to the idea that the universe as we see it is contained within a gigantic black hole, one that is so big that it contains all the stars, galaxies, and matter that we see around us.

One consideration in support of this theory is the observation made by Hubble's Space Telescope that confirmed recently that the expansion of the universe - caused by the Big Bang - is neither slowing nor remaining constant, but is instead accelerating. Current hypotheses about the cause for this acceleration include the presence of an exotic form of energy, called dark energy, which is causing the speeding up of the expansion of the universe. However, scientists have yet to find any evidence that such dark energy exists.

In the black hole theory of the universe, it could be argued that the accelerated expansion of the universe is leading all matter in the universe toward a black hole singularity since any object that falls into a black hole experiences increasing acceleration when falling toward a black hole's singularity - its center.

Another observation in support of the universal black hole theory is the fact that a large portion of the universe's mass seems to be missing and unaccounted for. While dark matter has been posited as the missing piece of the puzzle, it is also plausible that the unaccounted-for matter that is missing could be in the form of a black hole singularity that exists somewhere in the universe but which we cannot readily see yet due to large distances between it and the instruments we use to observe the universe. Since the bulk of a black hole's mass exists as a single point within the black hole when gravity compresses a great amount of mass into it, it could be argued that the majority of the mass of the universe exists as a singularity within the black hole universe in which we reside.

Overall, we may not be able to find the answers we seek even with extensive study into the observations we make of our universe. With current technology and the limitations imposed on us by the sheer size of the universe, we can only rely on indirect observations of phenomena to infer what is really happening in our universe.

input: Write a 3-paragraph blurb about why labradors make good pets. The text should be written from the point of view of an excited, loving lab named Max. He is trying to convince the reader that he would make a good pet. The text should be 500 words or less.
reference_output:
Hey! Hi there! My name is Max, and I am SO excited to tell you all about me and my breed and why we labs make the best dogs! So you might already know this about me and my friends because it's something we're sort of known for, but we have a LOT of energy! I love spending time outside, running around, and playing, and I love it even more when you play outside with me. I really like to go for walks every day, and I behave much better if you help me get my energy out in good ways instead of letting it build up. Sometimes when I have too much energy and I'm not getting enough exercise I get super bored. And then I start doing stuff I know I'm not supposed to - like chewing on things or barking and whining. I try hard not to, but it's just so hard because my energy has to come out somehow or else I'll explode!

I love going on hikes - I would go for a hike every day if I could! I like swimming too, so anytime you want to take me somewhere where I can get in the water is a perfect day in my book. I'm usually in a good mood and I mostly just want to spend time with my people - I would love to go to work with you or lay at your feet all day if you work from home. I really like kids too - they are so FUN to play with! Sometimes I have to be careful because I'm bigger than them and I might accidentally knock them over. I get so excited that it makes me wiggle around and jump and that's too much for some little kids. You might have to help me by reminding me to be gentle with them. Thank you so much. I really just want to make you happy but it's so hard sometimes because I get distracted when I'm excited. 

I don't want to brag but I'm also pretty smart. I can learn lots of tricks and I'm actually happier when you give me rules to obey and teach me how to do new things. I love making new friends - I would make new friends every day if I could. Making new friends might be my favorite thing ever! So you don't have to worry about me being mean to new people or anything like that. Do you have a lot of people at your house? That would be so cool if I got to hang out with your friends all the time. Or maybe you can take me with you when you go places? I don't exactly like being left at home all the time...I might get bored, kind of like I mentioned before. But I listen very well, so you can just take me with you wherever you go!

input: Write a short story about a snake called Susan and her friend, a turtle named Tanya. Susan and Tanya decide to go on holiday to escape the heat of the beach. The story should be upbeat.
reference_output:
Susan the snake was coiled on the beach under the beating sun, it was a very humid day, and the sun was relentless. "Oh, hi there," a little voice shouted from across the sand, it was Tanya the turtle, Susan's best friend. 
"This heat has got me beat my friend," said Susan, her scales sizzling in the mid-day sun. 
"You know what?" questioned Tanya, "Why don't we get out of here! I've heard of a magical land where it's never sunny or even warm! I think it's called Scotland." Susan turned to Tanya with a look of excitement brewing on her face and, with a hiss, she agreed. The two friends traveled across the scorching sand to an internet cafe and with the help of a local human, their flights were booked to the magical land of Scotland!

After a ten-hour journey, the plane door opened with a whoosh and blustery cold air billowed through the cabin. 
"Now, that's more like it!" exclaimed Susan, "I think we're going to like it here." And with that, the two friends embarked upon their adventure in the dreary gloam of Scotland's dismal weather, happy as could be.

input: What is Feng Shui?
reference_output: Feng Shui is an ancient Chinese practice of harmonizing individuals with their surrounding environment. This philosophy uses energy forces to create harmony between an individual and their environment. In interior design, Feng Shui is applied through the placement of furniture and objects to allow a smooth flow of energy, or "qi," within a space. This could involve orienting a bed or desk in a certain way, using specific colors, clearing clutter, or balancing various elements (wood, fire, earth, metal, water) to create a peaceful, balanced environment conducive to health and prosperity.

input: Help me brainstorm some personal qualities and characteristics that people should develop or strengthen in their lives to be more successful.
reference_output:
There are many personal qualities and characteristics that are advantageous for success. Here are a few that are excellent to focus on:

* Self-awareness: Developing self-awareness enables you to make deliberate decisions and actions aligned with your authentic self. By understanding your strengths, weaknesses, values, emotions, and motivations, you can focus on ongoing improvement and growth.

* Resilience: Cultivating resiliency helps you come back from a setback, adapt to change, and persevere through challenges. To develop resiliency, you must develop coping mechanisms, maintain a positive attitude, overcome, and learn from your failures.

* Integrity: It is necessary to adhere to moral and ethical principles to truly succeed in life. Integrity involves trustworthiness, honesty, and matching your words and actions. People respond to those with integrity and they are seen as reliable, dependable, and principled.

* Emotional intelligence: Skills such as empathy, self-regulation, social awareness, and effective communication enable you to recognize and manage emotions in yourself and others. It also enhances your relationships, promotes empathy, and improves your decision-making abilities.

* Humility: Humility allows you to acknowledge and value the contributions of others, be open to constructive feedback, and recognize your limits. Through humility, you foster collaboration and engage in continuous learning and personal growth.

* Open-mindedness: Open-minded individuals are accepting of new ideas, experiences, and perspectives. They are willing to engage in critical thinking, challenge their beliefs, and openly embrace diversity. Open-mindedness enables people to connect with others of different viewpoints.

* Adaptability: Being flexible and open to change and adjusting your plans and strategies as needed to allow for new situations is mandatory in an ever-changing world. Adaptability is crucial and adaptable individuals can thrive in a variety of environments and navigate difficult transitions.

* Creativity: The ability to think outside the box and come up with innovative solutions to problems helps you stand out and succeed in all aspects of your life. You can look through the world with a different lens and come up with unique answers to help the world.

input: I am having Easter at my home this year – we are inviting family and friends for a big feast. I love decorating for the holidays, but this is the one holiday I never know how to decorate for. Give me several ideas on decorating. Write 5 – 10 short paragraphs.
reference_output:
The first thing you can focus on is the color scheme of your home and decorations. For Easter, you will want to use seasonal colors for Spring. Pastel colors are usually associated with this holiday, including yellow, pink, blue, and green. You can also use fresh flowers and plants as this holiday and season are about rebirth.

These colors can be used anywhere in your home, but focus on the entrance area and the places your guest will see most. Put a lovely Spring wreath on your door or lay out a pretty pastel doormat to set the tone. Ensure the rooms where guests will spend the most time mingling and eating also receive this color treatment.
This can include decorative throw blankets and pillows, table settings, plates, napkins, and tablecloths.

Beyond color themes, colorful Easter eggs are at the top of the list, followed by bunnies and chicks. You can use these elements to make fun and colorful decorations such as a basket of colored eggs, a wreath made of eggs, or a centerpiece made in the shape of a bunny.

Finally, Easter is associated with sweet treats, specifically candy. A lovely Easter idea would be to have a sweet and dessert table. Include pastel-colored candies prominently. When you offer treats (like muffins or donuts) that do not meet the color scheme, consider presenting them on colorful plates or using Easter-themed cupcake picks to decorate the food.

input: Hello. I love European monuments but can't afford to travel there. Can you give me some places in the United State that have replicas of European monuments? I don't like large cities or a lot of glitz so don't include any replicas found in Las Vegas. And nothing in Atlantic City, New York City, etc. Preferably, the site will be similar to the ones found in Europe. Please tell me something about the replicas including where they are located. I'm not too interested in background information like who built it unless it is very unique or interesting. Put the information in a numbered list but use full sentences. Use the replica name and the location as a title over each list.
reference_output:
No problem! Here's a list of European monument replicas in the United States.

Leaning Tower of Niles, Niles, Illinois
1. The Leaning Tower of Niles is a replica of the Leaning Tower of Pisa.
2. The replica is about one-half the size of the original Leaning Tower of Pisa.
3. The city of Nile, Illinois bought the replica on November 17, 2015.
4. The city added four fountains and a pool to the replica.

Parthenon, Nashville, Tennessee
1. The Nashville Parthenon is a full-size replica of the Parthenon in Athens, Greece.
2. The Nashville Parthenon has an exact reconstruction of the Athena Parthenos statue lost in antiquity.
3. The Parthenon currently serves as a museum and as a location for outdoor theater performances.
4. The museum houses American artists of the 19th and 20th centuries. 

Stonehenge, Maryhill, Washington State
1. Maryhill Stonehenge is a replica of Stonehenge in England.
2. In 1929, Maryhill Stonehenge was completed to honor the soldiers who died in World War I.
3. Both the Maryhill Stonehenge and English Stonehenge align with the sunrise of the summer solstice.
4. It is under the umbrella of the Maryhill Museum of Art.

input: Please write a sad story about a kid named Kevin losing their dog. The story should be written for an audience of children. The story does not need an ending at this point. Write in the 3rd person.
reference_output:
Kevin and his dog were best friends. They ran together, played together, and slept together. Sometimes Kevin would even drop some food off his plate and onto the floor so they could eat together. Kevin was a bit shy, and he didn't make many friends at school. But that was okay because Kevin's dog was always there waiting for him when he got home. They'd go outside and throw the ball or go on walks. And then they'd come inside and curl up on the couch. Kevin loved his dog more than anything else in the whole world.

One day Kevin and his dog decided to go for a walk in the woods. Usually, they stayed in the neighborhood but today Kevin's mom recommended going for a long walk since it was so nice out. And Kevin knew that his dog would love to go explore the woods. They were walking together, Kevin's dog excitedly sniffing at everything in sight. Kevin loved how happy his dog was. Seeing his tail wag always made Kevin feel better.

They got to a turn in the trail when the dog suddenly stopped, pointing off into the woods. Kevin stopped beside him and looked out. "What do you see buddy?" Kevin asked. A slight crunch of the leaves and a deer shot out from behind a tree. Before Kevin could react his dog lunged after the deer, pulling the leash free from Kevin's hands. Kevin tried to hand on but he couldn't. He fell over and his dog took off into the woods after the deer. Kevin tried to chase after him but it was no use. They were way too fast.

Kevin began to cry. He screamed for his dog but it wasn't working. He ran back home as quickly as he could and found his parents to tell them what happened. They told Kevin they'd find him. They promised. And they went to go looking. It felt like forever passed but eventually, his parent came home. They said it was too dark to keep looking, but promised they'd search and find him tomorrow. But it didn't matter. Kevin knew it was his fault. He had let go of the leash too easily. He began to sob uncontrollably.

input: Write a short story (<3 paragraphs) about a dwarf child and an elf child becoming unlikely friends. Make it lighthearted and have a good message at the end.
reference_output: A long time ago, there was a dwarf named Gimli and an elf named Legolas. They grew up in the same town but were always told that they can't be friends. Dwarves and elves never got along, and their parents passed on this hatred to their children. Now Gimli and Legolas were gathering berries in the nearby woods when they ran into each other. They looked at each other awkwardly before scowling and going on to search for more berries. It quickly became a competition as each rushed along to find more berries than the other. They stayed within each other's view as they looked, going deeper and deeper into the woods. As they ran along looking for berries, they suddenly realized they were lost. They stopped to look, trying to find a marker or sign showing which way to go. They realized they had to work together to get out, and so they did. They looked around for what felt like hours before they came across a worn-out sign pointing toward a neighboring village. They cheered as they headed off quickly back into familiar territory. Gimli and Legolas learned that even though they looked different, they could work together and weren't that different after all. They got back to their village and told their story, changing the relationship the dwarves and elves had forever.

input:
Rewrite this recipe combining the ingredients and directions into a single narrative.  It should read like it is being presented on a TV cooking program.

Muffins With Goat Cheese and Chives 

Ingredients

Servings: 12 (makes about 12 muffins)

1/3 cup (80 milliliters) mild-tasting olive oil, plus more for brushing the pan

Generous 1 1/2 cups (200 grams) whole-wheat pastry flour (may substitute with white whole-wheat flour, or a generous 3/4 cups/100 grams of each all-purpose flour and whole-wheat flour)

2 teaspoons baking powder

1 teaspoon onion powder

1/2 teaspoon baking soda

1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper

1/4 teaspoon fine salt

2/3 cup (180 milliliters) whole or reduced-fat milk (may substitute with plant-based milk)

1/2 cup (113 grams) plain Greek yogurt

1 large egg

1 teaspoon honey

4 ounces (115 grams) fresh goat cheese (chevre), crumbled

1/2 cup (22 grams) finely chopped fresh chives


1/2 cup (22 grams) finely chopped fresh chives

Directions

Active: 25 mins

Total: 50 mins

Step 1
Position a rack in the middle of the oven and preheat to 375 degrees. Brush a nonstick muffin tin with olive oil.
Step 2
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, onion powder, baking soda, pepper and salt until combined.
Step 3
In a large bowl whisk together the milk, yogurt, oil, egg and honey. Add the flour mixture to the milk mixture and stir until just combined. Stir in the goat cheese and chives until just combined.
Step 4
Using a 1/4-cup (60-milliliter) measure, divide the batter among the muffin tin cups; each should be filled about three-quarters of the way.
Step 5
Bake for 18 to 20 minutes, or until the tops are golden brown and a toothpick or cake tester inserted into the center comes out clean.
Step 6
Transfer to a wire rack and let cool for 5 to 10 minutes, then run a small offset spatula or butter knife around the perimeter of each muffin before removing them from the tin. Serve warm.
reference_output:
This is a fresh take on muffins with some unexpected savory ingredients - goat cheese and chives.

For this recipe, you will need to set your oven to 375 degrees and position the rack in the middle. A nonstick muffin tin is recommended, but brush the tin with mild-tasting olive oil just to be safe.

The first step is to combine the dry ingredients in a medium size bowl. Start with one-and-a-half cups of whole-wheat pastry flour, one-half cups, at pastry flour. If you don't have it in your pantry, you can use white whole-wheat flour or three-quarter cups (100 grams) of all-purpose flour and whole-wheat flour. Two teaspoons of baking powder provide the leavening in this recipe. Follow this with one-half teaspoon of freshly ground black pepper and one-quarter teaspoon of fine salt. Briefly whisk the ingredients together to combine them.

In a larger bowl, whisk the wet ingredients: two-thirds cup (180 milliliters of whole or reduced-fat milk or plant-based milk; one half-up (113 grams) of plain Greek yogurt, one large egg, and one teaspoon of honey.  Once these ingredients are well combined, add the flour mixture. Stir the wet and dry ingredients together, but only until just combined.  

For the primary flavoring, gently stir in four ounces (115 grams) of fresh crumbled goat cheese, also sold as chevre and one half-cup of finely chopped fresh chives. 

After the batter is done, divide it equally between the muffin tin cups. Each cup should be about three-quarters full. Using a one-quarter measuring cup as a scoop is a good guide.

From here, the muffins go into the preheated oven. We are looking for golden-brown muffin tops, which should take about 18 to 20 minutes. You will know the muffins are done when an inserted toothpick or cake tester comes out clean. Transfer the tin to a wire rack to add in cooling. After five to ten minutes use a butter knife or offset spatula to remove the muffins.  

These tasty muffins are best served warm. Enjoy!

input:
Rewrite this article so someone brand new to the topic could understand it.

Define your vision.
You’ve been poetically musing over your song with pristine visions of grandeur and extravagance. Great! Write it down! This is all juicy information for the engineer and will provide nuance to the filter from which he hears and understands the song.
You’ve likely been working on a rough mix of your own with which you’re in a burning love/hate relationship. Wonderful! You’ll want to include this rough mix along with your files and clearly describe what you like and dislike about it. You want your song to sound a little bit like “Song X” and “Song Y” from your favourite artists and may have been, to a degree, using them as a northern star to guide your production process. Perfect! Include these in songs in your folder so that the engineer can examine their mix and work yours in a similar direction. What do you like about the way these songs sound? The bottom end? The way the vocals sit in the mix? Be descriptive about what moves you about their overall sound. Keep in mind that if your song doesn’t contain much similar sonic information as your reference tracks, a mix cannot sufficiently create these sounds or frequencies as they simply do not exist in your production. After you’ve exported a rough mix:
reference_output:
Define your vision:

You have been writing your song and refining it for weeks. You know exactly how it should sound and where you want it to go. Take note of this information. This is all information for the engineer and will provide them with a good place to start when getting your music ready for the radio. If you have already completed a rough mix of your song, you’ll want to include this along with your files and tell your engineer what works and doesn't work. If your song is similar to a song by a different artist, be sure to tell your mixing engineer. They can use this as direction when mixing your song. Explain to them what you like about that artist's music that they can bring out in your music. Keep in mind that if your music is not already similar to the other artist's music then it will be impossible for the mix engineer to effectively use it as a reference.

input: Please write me a short story about a serial killer. The story doesn't need an ending. Write the story in 1st person from the perspective of the killer. The story should have an ominous/unnerving tone.
reference_output:
I sat on the couch watching the morning news. Coffee on the table to my right, and a bowl of cereal in my lap. Nothing exciting, just some Cheerios with skim milk. The coffee black with a pinch of sugar and just a dash of Jack Daniels. It was a bad habit of mine, but everyone has their vices. On the TV, a breaking news alert flashed across the bottom of the screen as they cut to a field reporter standing near a local park. I reached for the remote and turned up the volume a few notches.

"... are reporting they've found yet another body. Again a young female with brown hair. The victim, Amanda Raser, was a middle school teacher. She leaves behind a loving family, a husband, and a number of students. A truly devastating tragedy as this spree of murders continues to ravage the community. Signs are beginning to indicate that this may be the work of a serial killer although the police urge the public not to panic. At this time the police report they have no leads or current suspects for the string of murders, but they assure you they are working tirelessly and feel a breakthrough is imminent."

The rest of the report faded into background noise as I leaned back in my seat. I grabbed my coffee by the handle and took a long sip. I set the mug down again on the coaster and a smile crept across my lips. They still had no suspects or leads, which meant I was in the clear. It may be harder now that everyone is on high alert, but I relished in the thrill of the fear I was causing.

input: Create a short blog post about the Spice Girls with an intro, 2 H2 subheadings and 3 H3 subheadings. Denote the subheadings by adding [H2] or [H3] next to them accordingly. Write in a fun, conversational tone from the perspective of a long-time fan.
reference_output:
I have been a fan of the Spice Girls since their inception, so I guess you could say that I'm a true 90s fangirl. When Scary, Sporty, Baby, Posh, and Ginger entered the girl band scene with their teen pop, I hopped on the bandwagon. I haven't looked back since! 
There are a lot of cool facts that people don't know about the Spice Girls, which is why I'm writing this post today. Follow along with me as I take you through the history of these girl power icons. 

The Early Days [H2]
The Spice Girls first got their start in 1994. It's wild to think that nearly 30 years have passed since then! The girls had to audition for the group, which led to the five members - Mel B, Melanie C, Emma Bunton, Geri Halliwell, and Victoria Beckham. 

First Steps [H3]
When the Spice Girls started, they wanted to pave their own path. They signed with Virgin Records and soon put out my favorite Spice Girls track called "Wannabe" in 1996. Did you know that the Spice Girls' first album sold over 20 million copies globally? They were truly a record-breaking girl group. 

Spiceworld Tour [H3]
By 1998, these girls were on a roll and ready to tour the world. Millions of people showed up to their shows, and hundreds of millions of dollars were spent on tickets. But sadly, Ginger Spice was ready to leave the band at this point.

Later Career [H3]
Despite the changes in the lineup, the Spice Girls continued to release some amazing songs, like the ever-famous "Goodbye". By the time the new millennium arrived, the Spice Girls were fizzling out, much to my chagrin. 

Looking Back at the Spice Girls Today [H2]
In the nearly three decades that the Spice Girls have existed, they've broken a lot of female pop star boundaries! The group has sold 100 million records globally, become one of the most successful British pop groups since the Beatles, and won numerous industry awards. 

These girls will forever inspire me!

input: Write a tutorial for a 50s makeup look but try to keep it within a one paragraph length
reference_output: Achieving the perfect 1950s makeup look can be simple. To do your base, it's important to use a natural-looking cream or liquid foundation for the skin. Keep it light and delicate, erring on the paler side. Top with a peachy or flesh-colored powder to keep the face matte. For your brows, a fuller, more arched look was popular at the time. Use natural tones for your eyeshadow, or omit it altogether- shadows were quite minimal for the period. Instead, opt for a soft liner on the upper lash line that blurs out into a slight wing. Finish the eyes with mascara. Blush should be placed high on the cheeks, and should never be placed lower than the tip of the nose. Go for a rose or pinky color. Finally, line your lips with a lip pencil and fill them in with a corresponding lipstick. Though reds shone in the '40s, this decade was all about pinks and purples and orange-toned reds.

input:
Please rewrite this text so that a middle schooler could understand the story.

Segregation was written into law; the front of a Montgomery bus was reserved for white citizens and the seats behind them for Black citizens. However, it was only by custom that bus drivers had the authority to ask a Black person to give up a seat for a white rider. There were contradictory Montgomery laws on the books: One said segregation must be enforced, but another, largely ignored, said no person (white or Black) could be asked to give up a seat even if there were no other seats on the bus available.

Nonetheless, at one point on the route, a white man had no seat because all the seats in the designated “white” section were taken. So the driver told the riders in the four seats of the first row of the “colored” section to stand, in effect adding another row to the “white” section. The three others obeyed. Parks did not.
reference_output: In Montgomery, there were laws about what we now call segregation. The law said black people had to sit on the back of the bus in the "colored" section, while white people could sit up at the front of the bus in the "white" section. Now, if a white rider got on the bus and it was full, the bus driver could ask a black person to give up their seat. This wasn't always true for all buses because the rules could be confusing. But on this day, when Rosa Parks rode the bus, that's what happened. The bus driver asked her and three others to get up from their seats for white riders who did not have a seat. The other three got out of their seats, but Rosa Parks did not.

“You’re not good enough” is all I heard. That wasn’t exactly what was being said, but it was clearly implied. The corporate jargon just attempted to soften the blow. I’d rather her just say it outright.

What am I going to tell my wife? She’ll ask how my day was, and I’ll get to gladly report that our accounts are about to plummet.

“…organizational restructuring… comprehensive analysis… strategic objectives…”

Her cold words exited her mouth with emotionless, corporate precision. For a second, I thought there’d be a light, until it came at last:

“Termination.”

reference_output:
Good evening everyone. I am very grateful to receive this recognition from the school tonight. To retire my number so that I may always be remembered is a great tribute. I thank you all so much for being here to share this occasion with me. I am very proud to have my hard work recognized in this way and it's a great achievement.

However, I did not accomplish this feat alone. I had many great teammates and we all worked hard together as a team. I would like to take this moment to thank them because I would not be receiving this great honor without them.
 
I would also like to thank the school's athletic director for arranging this halftime ceremony, as well as my wife and children for giving me the motivation to stay on the right track in life. My love for basketball gave me the motivation to be great on the court and my love for my wife and kids gives me the motivation to continue to be great in life.

 Last, but not least, thank you to my coaches for working with me and helping me get better and better at the game so that I could achieve this. It was a privilege to learn from them.  I have continued to put forth the type of effort they expected of me even though I no longer play basketball. Pushing yourself to your limit is a valuable mindset not only in sports but in life itself and I am very grateful to them for teaching me that.

To conclude my speech I would like to wish both teams good luck in the second half tonight and give a final thank you to the school for allowing me to receive such a incredible honor. I hope everyone has a good night and enjoys the rest of the game.
